schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The enforced form of CP1-CP6 in code: the frozen `CoreValue` record, the canonical `CORE_VALUES` tuple (the six, in order), and `CoreValuesMixin`. The base-most class of the whole spine — it composes no one and everyone composes it. `BaseDevice` and `BaseShim` mix it in, so every device and every shim carries the six structurally.
why: Law 2 — CP1-CP6 hold everywhere, including in the process that builds the system. A value enforced by prose is policy; a value carried by a class you cannot subclass without is physics (Law 4). Placing them here is necessary but not sufficient: a value is *consumed* only when a contract turns it into a check (CP1 -> no 'done' without an honesty gate). This module guarantees only structural presence — the consumption layer is per-device charter work.
invoke: import: `from cairn.base.core_values import CoreValuesMixin, CORE_VALUES`. Prove: `python3 cairn/base/proofs/test_core_values.py` (exit 0 = green).
entry: GRAFT (Law 8) — carried-over-intact from the quarry `UnseenUniversity/.../diagnostic_base/core_values.py` with its pin test, not re-derived. The core values are the one part of the predecessor that was never the problem (Law 2). Ratified by Akien as Cairn's values 2026-07-14.
state: PROVED for what n=1 covers (the contract itself: the six, in order, frozen, and matching the commons record). OPEN EDGE, filed not faked: the composition teeth — 'no device or shim can lack the values' — are a property of `BaseDevice`/`BaseShim`, which do not exist yet; they land with the next graft (base/device + base/shim). Casting that check green now would be hollow (Law 8).
gates: proof gate — the pin test in proofs/test_core_values.py, runnable bare (python3) today; the tester device will own how it runs when it lands. Durability gate (code-seam): not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: the code and the canonical commons record (`CairnCommons/intentions/core-values.md`) disagree on the set, the order, or any narrative — a red by physics, per that record's own falsifier. Also red: `CORE_VALUES` is not exactly [CP1..CP6] in order, or the frozen `CoreValue` becomes mutable.
how_it_learns: It does not learn autonomously — by design. This is a frozen, ratified contract (Law 2), the terminal floor the rest of the system stands on; nothing-is-terminal does not apply to the axioms themselves. Its horizon is a human one: the words change only when Akien re-ratifies them (see core-values.md horizon). What *does* learn is the consumption layer — each device charter answers, for itself, which CP became which enforced check, and whether that check earns its keep. The base only guarantees the six are present to be consumed.
traces_to: Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share an address)
Law 8 (entry by graft, with a proof a hollow build couldn't pass)
Telos 6.1 (CP1-6 pinned by tests, enforced in-process)
owner: Akien owns the words (ratification is his gate). CC owns the enforced form (the code + its pin test) and gates writes to it.
proof: proofs/test_core_values.py — asserts the six in order, the frozen record + lookup helper, and cross-checks the code against core-values.md (whitespace-normalized) so drift is caught by physics. Green: `python3 cairn/base/proofs/test_core_values.py`.
provenance: born 2026-07-17 as the founding stone of the code spine — the first class-space code cast, opening the CP/diagnostic base spine step (MAP.md build order) after CLAUDE.md/skills/launchers/commons. Grafted, not authored (see entry).
